title: Data, API, Algorithm, and Domain Invariants · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs data, api, algorithm, and domain invariants from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for executable domain and interface assurance suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.
Validate untrusted input for structure, semantics, authorization, limits, normalization, provenance, and version before trusted use.
State preconditions, invariant, termination, result, counterexample, complexity, scale boundary, and alternative for consequential algorithms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Domain Invariant · changed case · Review Domain Invariant
steps.instruction:
review case — Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language.

Which action demonstrates Domain Invariant without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Domain Invariant · changed case · Review Domain Invariant.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for formalize critical identities, states, quantities, relationships, transitions, and impossible conditions in domain language. For Domain Invariant · changed case · Review Domain Invariant, keep Domain Invariant connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Schemas and endpoints look complete while identity, authorization, concurrency, numerical, lifecycle, compatibility, and recovery invariants remain implicit. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. stimulus.title: Data Recovery Invariant case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove backup, restore, migration, replay, reconciliation, and deletion preserve or intentionally transform domain invariants. Artifact under review: executable domain and interface assurance suite.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Data Recovery Invariant, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Data Recovery Invariant

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove backup, restore, migration, replay, reconciliation, and deletion preserve or intentionally transform domain invariants.
